report_name: AI Fundamentally Reshaping Ransomware Landscape, Making Attacks More Sophisticated and Accessible

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: AI is used to generate highly convincing, personalized phishing emails at a massive scale, with contextually relevant lures and flawless grammar, making them more effective than traditional campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: AI is used to automate reconnaissance by scanning the entire internet for vulnerable systems in minutes.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The article discusses how AI is accelerating ransomware attacks, which use this technique as their primary form of impact.

attack_patterns.name: Polymorphic Code
attack_patterns.description: AI can be used to create polymorphic malware that constantly changes its code to evade signature-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: AI-optimized attacks are expected to make greater use of living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBAS) like powershell.exe, wmic.exe, and certutil.exe to evade detection.

tools.name: powershell.exe
tools.description: Mentioned as a Living-off-the-Land binary that AI-optimized attacks will likely use for execution.

tools.name: wmic.exe
tools.description: Mentioned as a Living-off-the-Land binary that AI-optimized attacks will likely use for execution.

tools.name: certutil.exe
tools.description: Mentioned as a Living-off-the-Land binary that AI-optimized attacks will likely use for execution.

identities.name: FBI
identities.description: A former FBI Cyber Deputy Assistant Director, Cynthia Kaiser, warned that AI is accelerating cybercrime and making it a key national security issue.

identities.name: Nozomi Networks
identities.description: A technology vendor whose expert, Michael Plante, commented on how AI changes the economics and timeline of exploitation.

identities.name: Cynthia Kaiser
identities.description: Former FBI Cyber Deputy Assistant Director who spoke at Infosecurity Europe 2026 about the impact of AI on cyber threats.

identities.name: Michael Plante
identities.description: An expert from Nozomi Networks who commented on the need for CISOs to rethink security models in response to AI-driven threats.

indicators.name: Anomalous LOLBAS Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous LOLBAS Execution
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBAS) from unusual parent processes, which could indicate AI-optimized attack behavior.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/feature/The-year-of-AI-2026-sees-influx-of-ransomware-attacks
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  filter:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\services.exe'
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common LOLBAS tools (PowerShell, WMIC, CertUtil) which the article suggests will be increasingly used by AI-driven attacks.

indicators.name: LOLBAS Execution IOC
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: LOLBAS Execution IOC
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-09876543210f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of specific living-off-the-land binaries mentioned as being leveraged by AI-driven attacks.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/feature/The-year-of-AI-2026-sees-influx-of-ransomware-attacks
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: IOC-based indicator for the execution of LOLBAS tools mentioned in the article.

indicators.name: AI-Enhanced Phishing Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential AI-Generated Phishing Email
id: 8c7b6a5f-4e3d-4c2b-1a09-876543210fed
status: experimental
description: Detects emails with characteristics that may suggest generation by AI, such as flawless grammar combined with urgent calls to action, as described in the context of AI-enhanced phishing campaigns.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/feature/The-year-of-AI-2026-sees-influx-of-ransomware-attacks
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1566
level: low
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    Body|contains|all:
      - 'urgent action required'
      - 'verify your account'
  filter:
    Header|contains: 'SPF: pass'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: A behavioral indicator for detecting phishing emails that may have been generated by AI, as described in the article.

indicators.name: AI-Driven Reconnaissance Scanning Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: High-Frequency Outbound Scanning
id: 7b6a5f4e-3d2c-4b1a-0987-6543210fedcb
status: experimental
description: Detects a high rate of connection attempts to a wide range of external IPs from a single source, which could indicate AI-driven automated reconnaissance.
references:
  - https://www.computerweekly.com/feature/The-year-of-AI-2026-sees-influx-of-ransomware-attacks
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1595
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'allow'
  timeframe: 10m
  condition: selection | count(dst_ip) by src_ip > 1000
indicators.description: A behavioral indicator for detecting rapid, automated scanning characteristic of AI-powered reconnaissance.
